core_mechanism: a memory-for-computation tradeoff in reverse-mode differentiation over a shared-prefix branching tree: the common prefix is evaluated once without retaining adjoint state, only the minimal downstream-relevant state is cached, and dependent branches are replayed/streamed one at a time so peak working memory stays bounded regardless of branch count
brief_reason: The abstract describes evaluating the shared prompt without autograd, retaining only model-specific state needed by later tokens, and replaying short response branches one at a time to shrink the live training graph in exchange for extra replay time, so that adding branches barely increases peak memory.
